focus: security: cross-cutting: secrets/log redaction, world-writable paths, TOCTOU, unsafe deserialization across all

title: Fan-out review subprocesses get repo-wide write power while isolation is only prompt-enforced
detail: Default fan-out CLI lineages are intended to write only under lineageDir, but missing sandboxMode resolves to workspace-write for Codex and acceptEdits for Claude, while OpenCode is launched with --dangerously-skip-permissions. The implementation states the lineage-dir boundary is enforced by prompt text. A prompt is not a security boundary, so a buggy or prompt-injected child can modify reviewed files or shared state outside the lineage artifact directory during normal fan-out review.
claim: Fan-out CLI lineages can write outside their intended artifact directory by default.
counterevidenceSought: Looked for path-scoped sandboxing, chroot/worktree isolation, or a caller-enforced default sandboxMode; found default write sandbox and prompt-only boundary in the command path.
alternativeExplanation: The comment may reflect an accepted product trade-off because current CLIs lack path-scoped write sandboxes, but that still leaves the advertised lineage boundary unenforced.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if every production fan-out caller always supplies a true path-limited sandbox or isolated worktree outside this module before fanout-run.cjs executes.
suggestedFix: Fail closed for review/context fan-out unless the executor can be constrained to the lineage artifact directory; otherwise run children in a temporary worktree rooted at lineageDir, pass a sandbox that cannot write the source repo, and copy only validated artifacts back.

title: Coverage graph metadata is accepted and re-emitted without redaction
detail: upsert.cjs copies arbitrary node/edge metadata into graph rows, the DB serializes it wholesale, and coverage query helpers parse and return the full metadata object to query.cjs responses. The sibling council query surface has an explicit sanitizeMetadata allowlist, which confirms the expected prompt-safe boundary exists elsewhere but is missing for coverage graphs. A normal graph event carrying token, api_key, copied .env text, or credentials in metadata will be persisted and emitted into query stdout for downstream prompts/logs.
claim: Coverage graph query output can leak secrets from arbitrary metadata.
counterevidenceSought: Searched for a coverage-graph metadata sanitizer and found only council-scoped sanitization; coverage query branches return raw parsed metadata.
alternativeExplanation: Producers may intend metadata to contain only non-sensitive fields, but that contract is not enforced by upsert.cjs or the DB/query layer.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all coverage graph producers validate metadata against a non-sensitive schema before invoking upsert.cjs and query output is never sent to prompts or logs.
suggestedFix: Add a coverage-graph prompt-safe projection that allowlists known scalar metadata keys and redacts common secret patterns before query output; keep full metadata only in storage if needed, or reject sensitive keys at upsert time.

title: Fan-out persists raw subprocess stdout for salvage without a redaction pass
detail: fanout-run captures child stdout up to 20 MiB, writes it verbatim to logs/fanout-lineage.out, and fanout-salvage can copy up to 50k recovered text into an iteration markdown file. If a child prints credential-bearing tool output or source content during a failed lineage, the salvage/debug artifacts retain it unredacted.
claim: Fan-out salvage/debug artifacts can persist raw child stdout containing secrets.
counterevidenceSought: Looked for a redaction helper between stdout capture, log write, and salvage write; the code writes savedStdout directly and salvage writes recoveredText directly.
alternativeExplanation: The artifacts are local and intended for recovery/debugging, so the operational exposure may be limited to repo artifact handling.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if lineage stdout is guaranteed by every supported CLI to contain only already-redacted assistant text and these logs are excluded from persistence/sharing.
suggestedFix: Run captured stdout through a shared redaction helper before writing logs or salvaged markdown; persist a bounded raw log only behind an explicit debug flag and outside review artifacts.
